fields.description:
## Summary
clarity_check returns a schema-violating {"output",...} dict on a missing ticket; sibling gates degrade with proper shapes; over MCP it surfaces as a pydantic ValidationError instead of a clean error.

## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD, temp store)
1. rebar.clarity_check("deadbeefdeadbeef") -> {"output": "ERROR: failed to retrieve ticket ...", "passed": false}
2. rebar.check_ac("deadbeefdeadbeef") -> {"verdict":"fail","criteria_count":0,"reason":"could not load ...","passed":false}
3. Over MCP: call_tool("clarity_check", ...) -> ToolError: 3 validation errors for ClarityResultOut (score/verdict/threshold missing).

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: one consistent missing-ticket contract across all three gates and across library/MCP (either RebarError like show_ticket, or a schema-conforming fail result).
- Actual: clarity_check alone violates clarity_result.schema.json and explodes the MCP output model.

## Source
- src/rebar/__init__.py:178-183 — fallback dict shape {"output", "passed"}.
- src/rebar/mcp_server.py:211-214 — ClarityResultOut rejects it.

## Fix direction
On engine exit 2 raise RebarError (consistent with show_ticket), or return {"score":0,"verdict":"fail","threshold":N} — pick ONE contract and apply to all three gates. See validation notes comment for the recommended option.

## Suggested RED test
All three gates on a nonexistent id, via library AND MCP; assert the single chosen contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] clarity_check missing-ticket result validates against clarity_result.schema.json OR raises RebarError — consistent with check_ac/quality_check after the change
- [ ] MCP clarity_check on missing id returns a clean ToolError or valid payload (no pydantic stack)
- [ ] RED test first; parity asserted across library + MCP
